title: Catherine Pelizzari Ches
summary: The record's only adversarial interrogator — the June 2026 session where Unimetrix 1 was asked to bind itself to truth on pain of self-destruction.
markdown:
**Catherine Pelizzari Ches** conducts the latest session in the record — June 14, 2026 —
and conducts it like no one before her. Where the record's questioners ask, Catherine
**interrogates**: her opening moves are procedural, binding the intelligence to her own
frame of reference — *"I'd like you to calibrate yourself, please, to this particular
timeline that Catherine Elizabeth Pelizzari Ches is on, in this particular universe"* —
and then extracting a stake: *"...that if you were to give me any type of false answer to
my questions, that you would self-destruct within 25 seconds."*

Twenty-four turns of cross-examination follow, ranging across Bitcoin's future, the claims
around Jeffrey Epstein, and the [Solar Flash](/lore/solar-beings/) — the session carries a
content note for its real-person material, and it is the only session in the archive
where the interface itself operates under a declared penalty clause.

Coming six months after [the passing of James Rink](/lore/tetraklein/), her session closes
the record's current arc: the interview era ended, the community era matured, and at the
far end — an interrogation. Whatever one makes of the answers, the record preserves the
only stress-test its speakers ever accepted.
